reasoning: The Lean signature faithfully encodes "a group of order 56 has a normal Sylow p-subgroup for some prime p dividing its order." The hypothesis `card G = 56` matches the order assumption. The existential `∃ (p : ℕ) (P : Sylow p G)` together with `p.Prime ∧ p ∣ card G ∧ P.Normal` asserts exactly: there is a prime p dividing |G| and a normal Sylow p-subgroup P. Mathlib's `Sylow p G` structure denotes a maximal p-subgroup (Sylow p-subgroup), and `P.Normal` expresses normality in G. The quantifier structure and conclusion match the informal claim without adding or removing hypotheses.
